COP = 3.28 as claimed in task description. Verified: 1 / 0.30488 = 3.28 exactly. This matches ASHRAE 90.1 minimum efficiency requirements for heat pumps (COP >= 3.2).
Mathematical verification: EIR = 1/COP, so COP = 1/0.30488. Verified via Python calculation.
Standard ASHRAE 90.1 Table 6.8.1B value for heat pump minimum operating temperature cutoff in DOE-2 models. Referenced in web search results and eQUEST documentation discussions.
Valid DOE-2/eQUEST HEAT-SOURCE parameter value. Current value in 4StoreyBuilding.inp is FURNACE; target is to change to HEAT-PUMP. Confirmed syntax matches eQUEST parameter naming conventions.
Valid DOE-2/eQUEST HP-SUPP-SOURCE parameter value for specifying electric auxiliary heat. Consistent with standard heat pump modeling practices.
Task description explicitly states 'run the full annual DOE-2.2 simulation' and verifier checks for .SIM file generation (10 pts for simulation ran). Verified as requirement.

Official release from doe2.com. Confirmed in environment setup scripts (install_equest.ps1) that downloads eQUEST_3-65_Build7175_2018-10-04.zip from https://doe2.com/Download/equest/eQUEST_3-65_Build7175_2018-10-04.zip
eQUEST 3.65 standard version uses DOE-2.2 simulation engine (DOE-2.3 available as option). Confirmed via env.json and web search results about eQUEST 3.65 features.

pi_items.verified_value: COP 3.28 meets/exceeds ASHRAE 90.1-2007 minimum of COP 3.2 at 8.3°C db / 6.1°C wb
pi_items.source: Web search for 'air-source heat pump COP 3.28 ASHRAE' confirmed ANSI/ASHRAE/IESNA 90.1-2007 specifies minimum COP 3.2 for heat pumps over 40 kW.

pi_items.metadata_value: Air-source heat pumps with COP 3.0-3.5 are typical
pi_items.verified_value: COP 3.28 falls within ideal range of 3.0-4.0 for air-source heat pumps
pi_items.source: Web search results indicate typical ASHP COP ranges from 2.0-4.0, with 3.0-3.5 generally ideal for residential applications. COP 3.28 is realistic.

privileged_info_summary: The heat_pump_conversion_middle_floor task targets the real 4StoreyBuilding eQUEST model with five actual HVAC systems (M.S21, M.E22, M.N23, M.W24, M.C25) currently configured with gas furnaces. The target parameters are based on verified standards: HEATING-EIR of 0.30488 corresponds to a COP of 3.28, which meets ASHRAE 90.1-2007 minimum efficiency requirements for heat pumps (COP >= 3.2). The MIN-HP-T value of 17°F is the standard ASHRAE 90.1 Table 6.8.1B cutoff temperature for heat pump compressor operation. The task requires execution of a full annual DOE-2.2 simulation using eQUEST 3.65 (Build 7175, official release from doe2.com). All five systems must be converted from FURNACE to HEAT-PUMP heat sources with electric supplementary heat. The building data is non-synthetic real eQUEST project data.
